description: SCHEDULER BUG: LR=0 after epoch 1. Invalidated.
24-dim, d=512/h=8/L=1/ff=256/lr=5e-4, wave 2 heads. Std wins slightly.
Gap widens at 3ep: standard pulls ahead. Wave features not helping.
Alpha=0.05 ASA bias HURTS wave model significantly.
Alpha=0.1 ASA bias even worse.
Alpha=0.2 still worse.
Alpha=0.5 still worse. ASA features harmful as attention bias.
ORBITAL ALIGNMENT FIX: moved QUERY dims 20-23→16-19 on requirements. Dot product nonzero: 2.5%→44.4%.
3ep with aligned features. Gap narrowed -0.028→-0.009.
5ep: gap narrowing trend. -0.036(ep1)→-0.016(ep5). Wave converging.
Aligned features: alpha still harmful.
Same.
Same. Fixed bias disrupts learned attention.
4 wave heads: way too many. Lost learnable capacity.
1 wave head WINS! +0.02 gap. Sweet spot: minimal structural intervention.
Wave boost: slight win. Learned Q/K + wave bias helps but weak.
1-wave-head 3-seed: wins 3/3 but t=1.6, p>0.10. Effect small (+0.42%).
5-epoch 3-seed: advantage ERASED. Mean gap -0.001, wave wins 1/3. Standard catches up.
Overlap decomposition: orbitals=71%, semantics=15%, query=0%. POS-level info dominates.
Halved orbitals: WORSE. Orbitals are the useful signal.
Hard mask 4w+4s: too many constrained heads.
Hard mask 2w+6s: still too many.
Hard mask 1w+7s WINS at 5ep! +0.028 gap HOLDS (soft wave erased by ep5).
3-seed: wins 2/3, mean +0.009. Not significant but BETTER than soft wave (-0.001). Hard mask holds better.
10 EPOCHS: gap STABLE at +0.027! Hard mask persists ep3-10. Cannot be learned away.
SIGNIFICANT: t=5.24, p<0.025. Hard mask wins 3/3 at 10ep. Gap +0.020 STABLE. WHITEPAPER CONFIRMED.
STACKING WORKS! Hybrid+FFN gate: +0.038 (vs +0.028 without gate). Features help BOTH attention AND FFN.
DEFINITIVE: Hybrid+FFN gate 10ep 3-seed. t=16.5, gap +0.029. STACKING CONFIRMED.
SCALES! d=1024: hybrid+gate +0.026 gap. 250K params saved (1.0%). Hard mask holds at larger scale.
TRANSFERS! WikiText-2: hybrid+gate +0.022. Hard mask advantage on harder text.
L=2: hybrid+gate +0.016. 119K params saved. Holds across depth.
L=4: hybrid+gate +0.016. 238K params saved (1.5%). Advantage stable across depth.
SIGNIFICANT at L=4: t=3.49, p<0.05, 3/3. Hard mask holds across depth.
L=4, 2 wave heads: +0.007 gap, 501K saved (3.2%). More savings, smaller quality gain.
Hybrid L=4 (15.3M) vs Standard L=6 (18.2M): gap -0.048. 16% fewer params, 33% fewer layers, 95% quality.
Hybrid L=6 BEATS Standard L=6: +0.012, 357K saved (2.0%). Advantage holds at 6 layers.
L=6 10ep: gap narrows to +0.001. Deep models close gap with training but never surpass hybrid.
L=4 10ep seed 42: gap +0.037.
L=4 10ep 3-seed: mean +0.015, wins 2/3, t=1.24. High variance. Advantage real but not stat sig yet.
ABLATION: Random features + POS mask + FFN gate = WORSE. Random noise cancels mask benefit.
Zero features + POS mask + FFN gate = BEST at 3ep. sigmoid(0)=0.5 regularizes FFN.
Pure POS mask (no feat, no gate): +0.012 at 3ep.
Pure POS mask 10ep seed 42: +0.029.
DEFINITIVE: Pure POS mask 10ep 3-seed. t=14.0, p<0.025. SIMPLEST ARCHITECTURE WINS.
L=4 pure mask seed 42: +0.022.
L=4 pure mask 3-seed: mean +0.010, wins 2/3, t=1.36. Direction consistent but high std variance.
R25 features (verb frames + morph + copula): +0.015 vs std. ≈ pure mask.
R25 features 10ep: WORSE than pure mask (2.838 vs 2.826). Features add noise to attention overlap.
R25 features via gate only: also worse than pure mask. Features hurt regardless of injection mechanism.
d=1024: R25 features ≈ pure mask (2.725 vs 2.724). Scale doesn't change the story. Mask is sufficient.
GPT-2 proportions (d=768, L=6, h=12, ff=768): POS mask +0.020, 689K saved (1.9%). SCALES TO PRODUCTION CONFIG.
GPT-2 10ep single seed: +0.026. But see 3-seed below.
GPT-2 d=768 L=6 3-seed: NOT SIGNIFICANT. t=0.35, p=0.76, wins 2/3. Gap within noise at L=6.
d=768 L=4: mask +0.012 vs std. Mask L=4 (29.3M) ≈ Std L=6 (36.8M) = 1.26× compression.
d=768 L=3 mask: 25.8M, val=2.474. 2× compress, 97.9% quality of Std L=6.
d=768 L=2 mask: 22.4M, val=2.539. 3× compress, 95.3% quality of Std L=6.
d=768 L=4 3-seed: wins 3/3, t=3.89, p=0.06. See 5-seed below.
SIGNIFICANT: d=768 L=4 5-seed. t=4.18, p=0.014, wins 5/5. POS mask +0.015 at PRODUCTION WIDTH.
WikiText-2 d=768 L=4: NOT sig. High variance (seed42 +0.08, seed7 -0.02). Complex text needs more seeds or epochs.
d=768 L=4 wave head sweep: 1w=+0.012, 2w=+0.001, 3w=-0.016, 4w=-0.011. 1 wave head optimal (replicates d=512).
d=768 L=4 FFN gate HURTS: -0.012 vs std (p=0.046). Features harmful at this scale via gate too.
Gate SIGNIFICANTLY worse than pure mask: -0.030, p=0.015. PURE MASK IS OPTIMAL.
d=768 L=4 seq=256: gap +0.018 (vs +0.012 at seq=128). Longer sequences grow the mask advantage.
d=768 L=4 seq=512 seed 42: gap +0.046. See 3-seed below.
SIGNIFICANT: seq=512 d=768 L=4 3-seed. t=6.51, p=0.023, wins 3/3. SEQ SCALING: 128→+0.015, 512→+0.036.
OVERTURNS L=6 NON-RESULT! seq=512 d=768 L=6: t=8.03, p=0.015, gap +0.090! Seq=128 was misleading.
seq=1024 d=768 L=6: gap +0.048. Still wins but gap drops from +0.090 — data-limited (only 8.5K seqs).
seq=512 compression: Mask L=4 (29.5M) BEATS Std L=6 (37M) by +0.036 = 1.25× with BETTER quality!
STRONGEST RESULT: WikiText-2 seq=512 d=768 L=6. t=11.38, p=0.008, gap +0.143! Long seq + real text = max advantage.
10ep seq=512 L=6: gap HOLDS at +0.045 (vs +0.061 at 5ep). At seq=128 10ep the gap collapsed to +0.001. Seq=512 is durable.
seq=512 L=6 wave heads: 1w=+0.076, 2w=+0.042, 3w=+0.053. 1 HEAD STILL OPTIMAL even at long seq.
d=1024 L=4 seq=512 (lr=1e-4): mask +0.027. Advantage holds at larger model width + long seq.
THE RESULT: seq=512 d=768 L=6 5-seed. t=8.15, p=0.001, wins 5/5, gap +0.106. PRODUCTION-SCALE VALIDATION.
CRITICAL ABLATION at seq=512 L=6: std=2.445, no_mask_wave=2.405(+0.04), POS_mask=2.369(+0.076), RANDOM_mask=2.356(+0.089). RANDOM MASK MATCHES POS.
POS vs random mask 3-seed: NO DIFFERENCE. t=1.35, p=0.31. Random wins 2/3. Benefit is SPARSITY REGULARIZATION, not linguistics.
Density sweep: 25%=+0.055, 47%=+0.081, 67%=+0.093(best), 78%=+0.055, 100%=+0.040. Optimum ~50-67% density. POS mask at 66% is sweet spot by coincidence.
5 random masks: POS(2.369) beats 4/5 randoms(mean=2.378). But POS is within 1 std. Density matters more than content — POS at 66% is near-optimal density.
